title: POST-H-017-C — Source archive manifest y checksums
pass_criteria: ReleaseSourceArchiveManifest schema is registered and validates generated outputs.
Source archive manifest reports forbidden_entries_total=0 for the current clean source.
Git archive entries are normalized through the same runtime/build/cache exclusion policy used by deterministic-source-archive-plan.
Runtime/build/cache entries such as outputs/, .devpilot/devpilot.db, .venv/ and node_modules/ are declared as forbidden.
Critical artifact SHA-256 checksums are generated for present source-controlled files.
POST-H-017-C is synchronized across README, runbook, backlog, source registry, project state, CLI registry and TCR v1/v2.
block_criteria: BLOCK if outputs/, .devpilot/devpilot.db, .devpilot/agent_sessions/, .venv/ or node_modules/ are included in the source archive manifest.
BLOCK if generated outputs/release/source_archive_manifest.json does not validate against ReleaseSourceArchiveManifest.
BLOCK if the command uses network, external APIs, remote execution, connector write or plugin execution.
BLOCK if POST-H-017-C is presented as the final verified release reproducibility pack before POST-H-017-D/E.
risks: ZIPs without .git cannot prove git archive HEAD literally; they use deterministic-source-archive-plan as auditable fallback.
Checkouts with historically versioned runtime artifacts require source-archive normalization before evaluating forbidden_entries_total.
Checksums are local integrity evidence and do not replace signing or supply-chain certification.
Checksum tamper verification and dirty-repo enforcement are intentionally deferred to POST-H-017-D.
